models: Create Ticket.js (Sequelize Model): Fields `id` (PK), `pedidoId` (FK), `codigo` (String, unique short ID or UUID), timestamps.
routes: POST /api/pedidos/:id/tickets: Receives `{ cantidad: number }`. Creates N tickets for the order.
GET /api/pedidos/:id/tickets: Returns all tickets for a specific order.
GET /api/tracking/:codigo: Public endpoint. Finds Ticket by code, includes parent Pedido, PedidoItems, and Cliente. Returns safe public data for the tracking page.

components: TicketPrintTemplate: A React component styled for 58mm/80mm thermal printers using CSS `@media print`. Contains Customer Name, Payment Status, Order Items list, and a QR Code.
GenerateTicketsModal: UI to specify the number of tickets to generate.
pages: Order View/Edit: Integrate GenerateTicketsModal and a 'Print Tickets' action (using browser native `window.print()` or `react-to-print`).
Public Tracking Page (`/tracking/[codigo]`): A responsive page for customers to view real-time order status, populated by `GET /api/tracking/:codigo`.

frontend: qrcode.react or react-qr-code (for rendering the QR code inside the TicketPrintTemplate)
react-to-print (optional, simplifies triggering print of a specific React component)
gsap (for smooth micro-animations on the UI and tracking page, using GSAP core/react integration)

failureModes.Database Errors: Handle unique constraint violations if a generated `codigo` collides (use secure random short IDs).
failureModes.Invalid Tracking Code: If the customer scans a QR with an invalid/deleted code, `/tracking/[codigo]` should show a user-friendly 'Order not found' page.

qualityAndDesign.Animations: Use gsap-core / gsap-react for micro-animations (e.g., page loads, state transitions in tracking page).
qualityAndDesign.UI/UX: Follow frontend-design skill rules: avoid generic aesthetics, ensure the customer tracking page feels premium, responsive, and polished.

testingStrategy.Backend: API integration tests to verify POST `/api/pedidos/:id/tickets` creates exact N tickets and GET `/api/tracking/:codigo` returns correct public data without leaking sensitive info.
testingStrategy.Frontend: Visual check of the `@media print` layout to ensure it fits a standard thermal printer width without overflowing.

tasks: Create TicketPrintTemplate with CSS print rules
Fetch data from public tracking API
